parsed_review.summary: CP00-799 (RP26 Enterprise SaaS Hardening, risk B, 40 units, RP26.P04.M03.S19-RP26.P04.M05.S14) is a descriptor-only UI-workflow + permission/audit-binding pack, and its descriptor-verified production_ready intent is well supported by everything inspectable at review time. Source was inspected via Read/Grep/Glob (not summary-only). Metadata is consistent across the prompt, review-baseline, review-request, manifest, registry binding (ENTERPRISE_SAAS_CP799_PACK_BINDING), committed contract, the live closeout-pack-plan, and next-pack-queue: CP00-799, risk B, 40 units, first RP26.P04.M03.S19 / last RP26.P04.M05.S14, upstream CP00-798, next CP00-800 / RP26.P04.M05.S15, flag enterprise_saas_ui_workflow_permission_audit_descriptor_verified. The live plan live_state (last closed CP00-798, next_subphase RP26.P04.M03.S19) and next-pack-queue (live_latest_pack_id CP00-798, generated_queue_preview_pack_id CP00-799) confirm CP00-799 is the correct next pack; source_ledger_sha 13c7ed65... matches manifest/plan/queue, and there is no gap/overlap with CP798 (S18->S19) or CP800 (S14->S15, verified at the plan tail). Deliverable distribution {claude_review:4,implementation:11,ui:18,security_audit:4,fixture:1,test:1,hermes_evidence:1}=40 was confirmed by direct count of the baseline units and equals ENTERPRISE_SAAS_CP799_REQUIREMENTS.deliverable_counts and the prompt; micro-title distribution {Primary Implementation Slice:4,Secondary Workflow Slice:22,Permission And Audit Binding:14}=40; required_section_rows map exactly (M03=UI_FOUNDATION_ROWS.slice(18,22)=4 correctly continuing CP798's M03 slice(0,18), M04=full 22, M05=slice(0,14)=14). No-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced in code: freezeResult spreads ENTERPRISE_SAAS_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION into every descriptor/packet/case-set, createCaseSet stamps each row descriptor_only=true/runtime_execution=false with raw/tenant/SSO/SCIM/secret payloads all false, and validateDescriptor rejects identity-runtime opening, cross-tenant routes, key/secret material, runtime permission eval/writes, audit writes, permission-decision-detail/audit-event-body exposure, UI-runtime/API-handler execution, real tenant data, and enterprise-trust claims; UI rows (loading/empty/denied/interaction/permission_badge/audit_hint) carry executes_ui_runtime=false. The two CP799 tests assert coverage.errors empty + by_deliverable equality + by_phase RP26.P04=40; descriptor validation empty + source_descriptor=EnterpriseSaasCp798ApiEvidenceUiFoundationDescriptor + authority booleans (claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true) + M03 rows that never execute UI runtime/write product state/leak unauthorized count/permission detail + M04 22 rows that never execute UI runtime/expose permission-or-audit body + Hermes emits_runtime_receipt=false + Claude read_only/promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false + handoff to CP00-800, plus contract promotion and cp788-cp798 historical retention with all 12 projection descriptors. index.js export* surfaces all CP799 symbols, the validator script scripts/validate-rp26-enterprise-saas-hardening-contract.mjs is retargeted to CP799 (reads the live plan with manifest plan_binding_snapshot fallback), the README has the cp00-799 entry, and the committed contract is current_pack/latest_pack=CP00-799, latest_projection.descriptor=EnterpriseSaasCp799UiWorkflowPermissionAuditDescriptor, projections cp788-cp799, validation valid=true/errors=[]. Authority boundaries hold: Claude is a read-only independent reviewer (review-request read_only=true, tools Read,Grep,Glob), not final approver, and Hermes is used only as a descriptor-only control-plane evidence/review-receipt pattern (receipt_shape descriptor_only_enterprise_saas_no_write, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not embedded product runtime - matching the stated authority_boundary. Review-hardening preconditions are satisfied: auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1447-byte valid non-empty JSON), model claude-opus-4-8 / effort max / read_only, tools Read,Grep,Glob with forbidden_tools_present=false, prompt 2522 bytes within the 25600 limit, source_inspection_mode=read_tools_used, summary_only_review_allowed=false, active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted=[], and no invalid review attempts. The pack does not over-claim: manifest production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor, descriptor_only=true, production_ready_descriptor_verified=false, and pack_level_claude_review.status=pending. Two non-blocking P3 observations are recorded (closeout finalization artifacts not yet present at review time, so the 10 validation commands could not be independently re-run by this read-only review - deferred to and deterministically gated by npm run closeout-pack:validate; and a cosmetic changed-file-scope.json double-classification carryover). No P0/P1/P2 findings.

findings.title: Closeout finalization artifacts absent at review time; the 10 validation commands are not independently re-runnable by this read-only review
findings.details: At review time docs/closeout-packs/cp00-799/ contains only manifest.json; the required_pack_artifacts command-evidence.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json are absent (Glob confirms manifest.json is the only file present, and artifacts/closeout-pack-claude-review/cp00-799/ has no raw-output.json or review-receipt.json yet). This is expected workflow ordering: the manifest is still status=in_progress with production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, production_ready_descriptor_verified=false, and pack_level_claude_review.status=pending / valid_review_receipts=[], so the closeout-finalization artifacts are produced after this review receipt. Because this reviewer is read-only with no Bash, the 10 expected validation commands (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, and git diff --check) could not be independently executed; their results are not yet captured in-repo. The prior pack CP00-798 closed with the identical observation (CP798-001). Pack metadata, implementation, tests, validators, contract promotion, and no-write/no-real-data boundaries are all internally consistent and independently verified here, so this is workflow ordering rather than a defect.
findings.recommendation: No action required before this receipt. During finalization, generate command-evidence.json recording all 10 required commands at exit_code 0, plus adjudication.md (Production ready after adjudication: yes) and construction-inspection.json (verdict PASS), then run npm run closeout-pack:validate to deterministically gate the production_ready flip. The independent command verification this read-only review could not perform is covered by that gate.
findings.post_review_disposition: Expected workflow ordering; finalization artifacts are produced after this receipt and are deterministically gated by closeout-pack:validate. Non-blocking; deferred to closeout finalization.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json cosmetically double-classifies docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md
findings.details: docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md is listed both under untracked_implementation_files (line 160) and under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (line 166). This is the same labeling carryover flagged for CP00-798 (CP798-002). The authoritative scope fields remain correct: active_pack_implementation_refs lists exactly the seven real pack files (contract, README, registry.js, service.js, validators.js, model.test.js, validator script), active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted=[], and the manifest worktree_transition_policy treats new-session-handoff.md as unrelated while only requiring docs/closeout-packs/cp00-799 and artifacts/closeout-pack-claude-review/cp00-799 as active untracked files (the pack's own evidence dir docs/closeout-packs/cp00-799/ is correctly listed once under untracked_implementation_files). No unrelated dirty file (progress-control-room files, workbook/* matter docs, the .docx, the ldip invalid-attempt fixture) is pulled into the pack's authoritative refs, so pack scope integrity and closeout are unaffected.
findings.recommendation: In the changed-file-scope generator, classify each untracked file as exactly one of active-implementation or unrelated so docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md is not listed under both. Cosmetic; no closeout action required for CP00-799.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking cosmetic observation in a generated review-harness artifact; authoritative scope fields are correct. Deferred to a later generator cleanup.
